---
title: "Quarto 1"
author: "Suhani Shah"
format: html
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(palmerpenguins)
```


```{r}
#| echo: false
penguins
```


```{r}
# Install packages if you haven't already
# install.packages(c("tidyverse", "palmerpenguins"))

library(tidyverse)
library(palmerpenguins)

# Clean the data by removing missing values in key dimensions
penguins_clean <- penguins %>%
  drop_na(bill_length_mm, flipper_length_mm, species)

# Create a beautiful visualization
ggplot(data = penguins_clean, 
       aes(x = flipper_length_mm, 
           y = bill_length_mm, 
           color = species, 
           shape = species)) +
  # Add points with transparency to handle slight overlap
  geom_point(size = 3.5, alpha = 0.75) +
  # Add linear trend lines for each species
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # Use custom hex colors matching standard palmerpenguins branding
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Adelie" = "darkorange", 
                                "Chinstrap" = "purple", 
                                "Gentoo" = "cyan4")) +
  # Clean up labels and text hierarchy
  labs(
    title = "Penguin Bill Length vs. Flipper Length",
    subtitle = "Dimensions for Adelie, Chinstrap, and Gentoo Penguins at Palmer Station",
    x = "Flipper Length (mm)",
    y = "Bill Length (mm)",
    color = "Species",
    shape = "Species",
    caption = "Data Source: palmerpenguins R Package"
  ) +
  # Apply a minimalist foundational theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  # Fine-tune specific aesthetic layout properties
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18, color = "#1a1a1a", margin = margin(b = 6)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, color = "#555555", margin = margin(b = 20)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "#888888", hjust = 1, margin = margin(t = 20)),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#ebebeb"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12), face = "bold", size = 11, color = "#333333"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12), face = "bold", size = 11, color = "#333333")
  )

```
